entity.summary: Seychelles-based centralized exchange launched in 2017 and still operating under the KuCoin brand.

events.title: KuCoin began operating in 2017
events.description: KuCoin was founded and began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange in 2017.
events.notes: The canonical entity stores the launch at year precision; this event preserves that precision.

events.title: KuCoin disclosed a major hot-wallet security breach
events.description: KuCoin disclosed that private keys associated with hot wallets had been compromised and that large quantities of crypto assets were transferred out of the exchange. The exchange remained active and later reported substantial recovery and insurance coverage.
events.notes: The incident did not produce a terminal status change.

events.title: KuCoin pleaded guilty in a U.S. unlicensed money-transmission case
events.description: Peken Global Ltd, operating as KuCoin, pleaded guilty to operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business, agreed to more than $297 million in fines and forfeiture, and accepted a two-year exit from the U.S. market.
events.notes: The action was jurisdiction-specific and did not end KuCoin's global exchange operation.

evidence.title: KuCoin official exchange
evidence.notes: Current official exchange site confirming continued operation.

evidence.title: US charges KuCoin crypto exchange with anti-money laundering failures
evidence.notes: Independent reporting identifying KuCoin as founded in 2017.

evidence.title: The Largest Cryptocurrency Hacks So Far
evidence.notes: Secondary incident summary describing the September 2020 compromise and approximately $281 million in stolen assets.

evidence.title: KuCoin pleads guilty, agrees to pay nearly $300 million in US crypto case
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous reporting of the guilty plea, monetary resolution and two-year U.S. market exit.
